creator: yuri@freebsd.org
text:
> The updated patch isn't necessary; the script already exits with return code 1 if either fetch fails.

No, it doesn't pass an error out properly.
Consider this script demo.sh:
> #!/bin/sh
>
> fail() {
>   echo "failing"
>   return 1
> }
>
> succ() {
>   echo "succeeding"
>   return 0
> }
>
> fail
> succ

Run it:
> $ ./demo.sh 
> failing
> succeeding
> [yuri@yuri ~/tmp]$ echo $?
> 0

Part of it failed, yet it returns success code.
